message:
test(fcp-manifest): pin signatures.transparency_log_entry in golden manifest fixture

Adds a [signatures] block to the fcp-manifest golden TOML fixture and
refreshes the corresponding insta snapshot. Pins the canonical wire
shape that signed connector manifests are expected to carry once the
publisher-signature and transparency-log path is wired through.

Fixture (crates/fcp-manifest/tests/manifest_golden.rs):

- New [signatures] section with `transparency_log_entry =
  "objectid:cc...cc"` (the placeholder ObjectID used elsewhere in the
  golden fixture for fixed-content references). The block sits between
  the `[capability_constraints]` policy and `[policy]` blocks where
  the structural ordering of the manifest puts it.

Snapshot (crates/fcp-manifest/tests/snapshots/manifest_golden__full.snap):

- Replaces the previous `"signatures": null` line with the four-field
  serialised SignatureBundle:
  * publisher_signatures: []
  * publisher_threshold: null
  * registry_signature: null
  * transparency_log_entry: "objectid:cc...cc"
- This is the canonical zero-publisher / single-tlog-anchor shape that
  the deserialiser produces when only the transparency log entry is
  present in the source TOML; the other three fields default to their
  empty/None forms.

Together, the fixture + snapshot pair pins the shape so a regression
that drops, renames, or reorders the SignatureBundle fields surfaces
immediately on `cargo test -p fcp-manifest`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -156,6 +156,9 @@ unit = "requests"
 enforcement = "hard"
 scope = "credential"
 
+[signatures]
+transparency_log_entry = "objectid:cccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccccc"
+
 [policy]
 require_transparency_log = true
 require_attestation_types = ["in-toto", "reproducible-build"]
